title: OECD Social Expenditure Database (SOCX) — Aggregate Public/Private Social Expenditure as % of GDP
source_org: OECD (Employment, Labour and Social Affairs Directorate, Social Policy Division)
time_coverage: 1980–2024, annual (some 2022-24 values are OECD estimates per SOCX methodology)
license_terms: OECD Terms and Conditions — free reuse with attribution
notes: SDMX dataflow OECD.ELS.SPD:DSD_SOCX_AGG@DF_SOCX_AGG(1.0), 'Social expenditure aggregates'. Filtered to task scope 'as % of GDP': UNIT_MEASURE=PT_B1GQ, SPENDING_TYPE=_T (cash+in-kind total), PROGRAMME_TYPE=_T (all social policy areas total), PRICE_BASE=_Z (not applicable, since it's a ratio). EXPEND_SOURCE left unfiltered, so rows cover all 7 expenditure-source breakdowns: ES10 Public, ES10_20 Public+mandatory private, ES20 Mandatory private, ES20_30 Mandatory+voluntary private, ES30 Voluntary private, ES40 Net public, ES50 Net total. The unfiltered full dataflow (all programme/spending-type/source combinations, all units) is enormous (>150k rows for just one year across 12 countries) so was deliberately narrowed to the % of GDP headline series per the task brief; the full detailed breakdown was not downloaded. Some country-year cells are legitimately missing (OBS_STATUS=M, 'data cannot exist') e.g. Spain ES20 (mandatory private) pre-2000s.
